assumptions: An Observability Platform tenant exists or can be obtained through Chronosphere's commercial onboarding.
Prometheus metrics are already ingested and visible to the developer.

developer_goal: Authenticate to an existing Observability Platform tenant and run a first Prometheus metric query with Query Builder.

first_success.outcome: The developer selects an available Prometheus metric and runs a query in Metrics Explorer.
first_success.observable_signal: The Results section displays matching series and values in Table and Graph.

route_selection.surface: Chronosphere developer metrics query with Query Builder
route_selection.rule: Select the lexicographically shortest complete official route to the same first-success outcome after fixing product intent; if peer routes remain consequential, mark needs-human-judgment.

candidates.name: Chronosphere developer metrics query with Query Builder
candidates.reason: Recorded as a reconstructable candidate, but public evidence does not close signup-field opacity and/or peer-route lexicographic selection for a verified count.

candidates.name: OpenTelemetry Collector
candidates.reason: Docs recommend Chronosphere Collector for Prometheus discovery/scrape workloads.

candidates.name: Kubernetes DaemonSet Collector
candidates.reason: Recommended inside Kubernetes, but requires a cluster; standalone is faster when Kubernetes is not already available.

candidates.name: Administrator Collector ingestion
candidates.reason: This requires SysAdmin access, service-account credentials, Collector installation, and telemetry configuration; the policy explicitly prefers the first developer path.

required_path.action: Complete Chronosphere account creation or trial signup at the official entry URL.

required_fields.label: Signup identity and required account fields
required_fields.notes: Public evidence does not establish the complete current required field set behind the client-rendered or authenticated signup surface.

required_path.observable_result: A new Chronosphere account or trial session exists.

required_path.action: Complete any required email or identity verification for the new account.
required_path.observable_result: The account becomes usable for developer onboarding.

required_path.action: Complete the documented post-login setup for the selected Chronosphere candidate through first success.

required_fields.label: Candidate-specific configuration
required_fields.notes: Exact fields depend on unresolved product, auth, or framework selection.

required_path.observable_result: The Results section displays matching series and values in Table and Graph.

prerequisites.description: {'order': 1, 'type': 'access', 'requirement': 'Access to an organization-specific Chronosphere Observability Platform tenant.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S1']}
{'order': 2, 'type': 'permission', 'requirement': 'Developer permission to use Metrics Explorer and query at least one metric.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S4', 'S5']}
{'order': 3, 'type': 'environment', 'requirement': 'Prometheus metrics are already ingested into the tenant.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S1', 'S5']}

external_gates.description: A provisioned tenant is required; public docs start after authenticate rather than self-serve signup fields.
Creating the service-account API token requires SysAdmin team membership.

unavoidable_waits.description: Email or identity-provider verification delivery when the selected account route requires it.

platform_outcomes.description: Query telemetry data with Query Builder.

excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

uncertainties.question: How is a new Observability Platform tenant provisioned for a developer with no existing Chronosphere access?
uncertainties.impact: The path assumes an existing tenant and authenticated session before Collector install.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Resolve against current official docs and a dated fresh-account or cold walkthrough: Public get-started docs begin at authenticate and do not publish a self-serve tenant signup sequence.

uncertainties.question: What exact current required signup, identity, legal, and entitlement fields does Chronosphere show for a new developer, and which peer route is the lexicographic shortest path?
uncertainties.impact: Without those fields and a closed route choice, action and field counts cannot be verified for peer comparison.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated read-only walkthrough of the live signup and the selected post-login first-success path, with screenshots of required fields and confirmation that peer routes are longer or gated.
